candidates.stmt:
We’re proud to introduce Daniel Matchett, the former Reform UK Parliamentary Candidate in the 2024 General Election and Chairman of Reform UK Rossendale and Darwen, who is now standing to represent Whitworth and Bacup in the Lancashire County Council Elections on May 1st, 2025.

Daniel is a passionate advocate for real change in our communities. His journey reflects the frustration many feel with the current political landscape:

"In my opinion, Labour is not the answer anymore. I used to vote Labour once (when it was more centre-left), then Conservative in 2019, but now I stand for Reform UK. My politics has never changed; it’s the parties that have changed."

Daniel’s commitment is rooted in common-sense principles that resonate with ordinary people—values like fairness, accountability, and putting the needs of local communities first. He’s not afraid to speak the truth and challenge the status quo:

"We are on the cusp of a revolution in politics. Be a part of it—vote for a party that stands for common-sense principles. That’s what Whitworth and Bacup want—so vote Reform UK. Believe me when I tell you this, there’s more of you than you think! You’re just seeing the tip of the iceberg. It’s time for something different! And there will be surprises!"

Daniel isn’t just about words—he’s focused on action. He’s determined to address the local issues that matter most to Whitworth and Bacup residents:

Tackling the Influx of Potholes that damage vehicles and endanger road users

Challenging the Rise in HMOs (Houses of Multiple Occupancy) and their impact on local communities

Opposing the Scout Moor 2 Project, which threatens to affect the region’s natural landscape

Addressing Concerns About the Waste Disposal Site in the Centre of Bacup

"There is so much to do. Locally, there is so much to do. These are all issues that I plan to challenge—and will challenge—regardless of the election outcome. Unlike some of the current elected politicians who simply follow the party

A local resident with a clear vision for change, Bradley is stepping forward to represent a community he knows, lives in, and cares deeply about.

“As a resident of Darwen South, I see the potential in our ward, but I also see the neglect. From the state of our residential roads to the lack of visible policing around our town, the current system is failing us.”

Bradley is standing for Reform UK with a straightforward goal: to bring common sense back to the Town Hall. That means focusing on the issues that matter most to local people, starting with protecting valued community assets like Bold Venture Park and ensuring Darwen South remains a place residents are proud to call home.

“Darwen is a fantastic place to live, but for too long, our voice has been ignored. I aim to challenge the ‘business as usual’ culture at Blackburn with Darwen Council.”

His priorities are rooted in everyday concerns shared by residents across the ward. Bradley has pledged to:

Fight for lower council tax

Improve maintenance of local streets and infrastructure

Oppose wasteful ‘vanity projects’ that deliver little for Darwen residents

Prioritise local people for local services

Push to reopen the local Police Station

More than anything, Bradley wants to be a councillor who listens.

“I want to represent the ‘silent majority’, the families concerned about school places and the pensioners worried about rising bills. I’m not a career politician, I’m a neighbour who believes Darwen South deserves a representative as gritty and determined as the town itself.”

Bradley is positioning himself as a visible, straight-talking candidate who will stand up for the values, heritage and future of Darwen.

“My promise to you is simple...I will be a councillor who is present, who listens, and who always puts the residents of Darwen South first, every single time.”

As the elections approach, voters in the ward of Darwen South will have a clear choice and Bradley Langford is making his case to be the st

Denise lives in Darwen and works in Blackburn as an SEO Content Manager. She is a lone parent with one son who attends secondary school in Blackburn. Denise was educated at Loreto Covent Grammar School in Altrincham, specialising in English Language, History, and Theatre Studies. Following this, she gained a Degree in Media and Performance and, subsequently, a Master's Degree in Creative Writing from the University of Salford. She also studied at the University of Bolton, receiving a Diploma in Teaching English Literacy in the Lifelong Learning Sector. In addition to being an SEO Content Manager, Denise is a singer/songwriter and an advocate for the arts in education and the community. She believes creative subjects should be viewed as essential components of education and be funded as such. Another important issue Denise is keen to address is the financial and practical support given to carers who care for their family members, including those who care for children and adults who will require lifelong care. She also wants increased support and funding provided to children and adults with SEN, in educational, community, and domestic settings. Denise is vegan, passionate about the environment, and is an ardent supporter of animal rights and welfare. She is volunteer member of Keep Darwen Tidy and also supports the local cat rescue, English Barn Cats (EBC), by fostering abandoned, injured, and feral cats rescued by EBC. She has further focuses which include, the need for an increase in available social housing, funding for increased and easily accessible adult and children mental health support, and last, but by no means least, women's rights.

I am passionate about renewable energy generation and the green transition to a net-zero grid. In my day job I am exposed to many investments into early stage business working to build a sustainable future, and I can see the genuine excitement and interest across society and the economy in responsible net-zero technologies and innovations.

Furthermore I am absolutely in opposition to Israel's ongoing genocide in Palestine and Lebanon. This brutal campaign must end and we must ensure that money from our local economy is not flowing to the Israeli war machine.

I am a supporter of Trans rights and refute the "gender critical" ideological campaign against Trans people. Those who are transgender deserve to have their identity respected, to be treated with dignity, and to have their rights upheld.

In a previous career I worked as a Runner and Assistant Director in film and television productions across the north, productions which shone a spotlight on our region's outstanding landscapes and talent. I remain greatly passionate about supporting unions and worker's rights.

In my free time I like to run in Darwen's Sunnyhurst Woods, and enjoy playing and teaching tabletop games. I am originally from Newcastle Upon Tyne and have lived in many towns and cities across the north, and advocating for Northern communities is crucial to my politics.

Dear Resident,

I am writing to introduce myself as your Conservative candidate for Livesey with Pleasington.

I grew up in this ward, attended Feniscowles Primary School, and still live here with my family. This is not just somewhere I campaign — it is home.

I have served on Livesey Parish Council for a number of years, and that has taught me that good local representation means more than simply holding a position. It means being visible, speaking up for residents, challenging poor decisions, and making sure our area is not overlooked.

I believe Livesey with Pleasington deserves exactly that.

Our community needs someone who will stand up for safer roads, back local schools, support young people into practical opportunities, and make sure our area gets its fair share instead of being expected to pay in and stay quiet.

I have always believed that when something is not right, it should be challenged. That same approach is what I would bring to Blackburn with Darwen Borough Council: clear local representation, strong community backing, and a determination to get results for residents.

I also believe being a councillor should mean being accessible. So if there is something you would like to discuss, I would be very glad to hear from you. I am happy to visit you, speak on the phone, or be contacted by WhatsApp or email, whichever suits you best.

If elected, I will work hard, speak up for our ward, and treat the role as a responsibility to the people who live here.

I would be honoured to have your support.

Yours sincerely,
Rick Moore

I’m standing as your Reform candidate for Mill Hill & Moorgate because our community deserves real change — not empty promises.

For too long, people here have felt unheard, overlooked, and taken for granted. I want to change that. My commitment is simple: to listen properly, to be present in the community, and to represent your voice honestly.

I won’t pretend I can fix everything overnight — no one can. But what I can promise is this: I will always fight for what matters to you, I will always speak the truth, and I will never shy away from standing up for our area.

I’m not in this for the expenses, and I will refuse to sign up to policies that don’t serve the people of this community. Public service should be about integrity, not personal gain.

This campaign is about rebuilding trust, bringing people together, and ensuring Mill Hill & Moorgate receive the attention and respect they deserve.

If you have concerns, ideas, or just want to talk, I’m always open — you can reach me directly at stephenhartley@reformukblackburn.com

Let’s make a real difference, together.
